## Step 2: Make Prediction - Compilation Error

The test only uses already-imported symbols (`spawnSync`, `expect`) and the CLI runs in a subprocess. There is no new function to compile against, so I expect no compilation error — the test will go straight to a runtime/assertion error. Let me reframe.

Actually, since the test calls into the CLI subprocess and uses already-imported test utilities, there will be no compilation error. The test should compile successfully. The failure will be at runtime — the CLI currently doesn't validate "more damage entries of a type than insured", so it will succeed (status 0) and produce results, causing an assertion failure.

Let me run the test first to verify whether there's a compilation error or not.

```

- Expected: No compilation error (uses existing imports only)
- Reason: Test only uses already-imported `spawnSync` and `expect`; no new function/symbol introduced
- Error: None expected at compile time
```
